title: pseuderanthemum asperum
intro: Pseuderanthemum asperum, a species of flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae, is also known as the rough false antheum. It is a shrub-like plant native to tropical regions, and its leaves are used in traditional medicine. The plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which contribute to its health benefits such as wound healing and the treatment of fever and respiratory issues. In herbal preparations, Pseuderanthemum asperum can be used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, although further research is needed to confirm its effectiveness and safety for human consumption.
